report_name: Automotive Data Firm Autovista Confirms Ransomware Attack Causing Service Disruptions

attack_patterns.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
attack_patterns.description: A ransomware payload was deployed to encrypt critical systems at Autovista, causing widespread service disruption.

attack_patterns.name: Inhibit System Recovery
attack_patterns.description: The ransomware likely deleted volume shadow copies using 'vssadmin.exe delete shadows' to prevent system recovery, a common ransomware tactic mentioned for detection.

attack_patterns.name: Clear Windows Event Logs
attack_patterns.description: The attackers may have used 'wevtutil.exe' to clear Windows Event Logs to cover their tracks, based on pre-extracted cyber observables for detection.

tools.name: vssadmin.exe
tools.description: The tool vssadmin.exe was likely used by the ransomware to delete volume shadow copies, inhibiting system recovery.

tools.name: wevtutil.exe
tools.description: The tool wevtutil.exe was likely used to clear Windows Event Logs as a defense evasion technique.

campaigns.name: Autovista Ransomware Attack
campaigns.description: An unattributed ransomware attack in April 2026 that targeted automotive data firm Autovista, causing significant disruption to its client-facing applications across Europe and Australia.
campaigns.objective: Disrupt services and likely extort a ransom from Autovista.

identities.name: Autovista
identities.description: A leading automotive data and analytics firm, owned by J.D. Power, that was the primary victim of the ransomware attack. The attack disrupted its services, including brands like Eurotax, Glass's, Rødboka, and Schwacke.

identities.name: J.D. Power
identities.description: The parent company of Autovista, which acquired the firm in 2024.

indicators.name: VSSAdmin Shadow Copy Deletion
indicators.pattern:
title: VSSAdmin Shadow Copy Deletion
id: 2b1a9c8d-7e6f-4b5a-9d3c-1f8a7b6e5d4c
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of vssadmin.exe to delete volume shadow copies, a common technique used by ransomware to inhibit system recovery.
references:
  - https://www.theregister.com/2026/04/15/autovista_ransomware_attack/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-16
tags:
  - attack.t1490
  - attack.impact
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\vssadmin.exe'
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'delete'
      - 'shadows'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the use of vssadmin.exe to delete volume shadow copies, a common ransomware behavior mentioned in the report's detection section.

indicators.name: Windows Event Log Cleared with Wevtutil
indicators.pattern:
title: Windows Event Log Cleared with Wevtutil
id: 9e8d7c6b-5a4b-4c3d-2e1f-0a9b8c7d6e5f
status: experimental
description: Detects the use of wevtutil.exe to clear system event logs, a common defense evasion technique.
references:
  - https://www.theregister.com/2026/04/15/autovista_ransomware_attack/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-16
tags:
  - attack.t1070.001
  - attack.defense_evasion
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\wevtutil.exe'
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'cl '
      - 'clear-log'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the use of wevtutil.exe to clear event logs, based on pre-extracted cyber observables for detection.

relationships.source_name: Windows Event Log Cleared with Wevtutil
